title: oxalis purpurea
intro: Oxalis purpurea, also known as purple wood sorrel or lipstick plant, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat kidney stones and urinary tract infections due to its high concentration of oxalic acid. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, with its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties providing relief from these conditions. Oxalis purpurea can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and support overall urinary health.
